title: ferray-core: ops.rs operators don't broadcast — diverges from NumPy behavior
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

In NumPy, a + b broadcasts automatically: np.array([1,2,3]) + np.array([[10],[20]]) produces shape (2,3). In ferray-core, the std::ops overloads (Add, Sub, Mul, Div, Rem) require identical shapes and return an error otherwise. Broadcasting is deferred to ferray-ufunc as separate functions. This is a deliberate design choice but it means the most natural syntax (a + b) doesn't work for the most common NumPy pattern. Consider: (1) making operators broadcast by delegating to ferray-ufunc, or (2) at minimum providing a broadcasting_add(&a, &b) method on Array itself, so users don't need to import ferray-ufunc for basic arithmetic.
